hero.headline: Grow as a Musician. The Piano Becomes a Byproduct.
hero.subheadline: Intermediate & Advanced Piano Program – Where Theory, Ear Training, Transcription & Rhythm Shape Complete Musicians, Not Just Players.

usps.title: Musician First, Pianist Second
usps.description: We focus on growing you as a complete musician - with theory, ear training, transcription, rhythm, and notation. Piano skills become a natural byproduct of musical understanding.

usps.title: Learn Everything by Ear
usps.description: No memorization. You'll know the sixth of Bb major is G, hear it's a major sixth, and understand how it relates to surrounding notes - all by ear, applied to real songs.

usps.title: Music Factory: Deep Song Analysis
usps.description: Our signature transcription class where you analyze entire songs - notation, chord logic, rhythm structure. You'll understand WHY composers chose each chord, not just WHAT they played.

usps.title: Music Gym: 3D Practice System
usps.description: Dedicated exercises targeting ears, technique, and theory. Structured practice that builds genuine skill, not just muscle memory.

usps.title: Modular Learning Pathways
usps.description: 3-4 lesson modules with clear goals: singing & playing, chord inversions, gospel music, seasonal songs. You always know exactly what you're working toward.

usps.title: Rhythm Theory Included
usps.description: Unlike typical courses, we teach rhythm as theory - a complete system often missing from music education. Learn Konnakol, Afro-Cuban patterns, and complex metres.

usps.title: Graded Certificate Program
usps.description: This is serious music education. Weekly homework, personalized feedback, grading, and exams. For students who want to become musicians, not hobbyists.

usps.title: HD Recordings + Live Notes
usps.description: Every class recorded in HD quality. Plus handwritten notes, notation, supplementary resources, and practice tracks - lesson after lesson.

usps.title: Flexible Time Zones & Scheduling
usps.description: Weekday mornings, evenings after work, or weekend slots. Choose from 32, 40, 50, or 65-hour packages over a 6-month semester that fits your schedule.

pianoSkills.title: Advanced Piano Technique
items: Swara variations with transposition & intervallic motion
Spider exercise for finger independence & chromatic mastery
Exploring thirds (major/minor) across all keys
Pendulum patterns with scale fluency & finger crossing
Highest-note inversions with melodic voicing
Oom-Pah left-hand grooves with agile right-hand melodies

theory.title: Harmony & Advanced Theory
items: Chord shapes using the Circle of Fifths (all 12 keys)
Diatonic seventh chords & all seventh-chord families
Pivot thirds in major, harmonic minor, Dorian & melodic minor
The Mother of All Chord Progressions (diatonic circle)
ii-V-I progressions with block, Latin & arpeggio approaches
Modes: Mixolydian improv with scale-building & plagal cadences

earTraining.title: Ear Training & Transcription
items: Identifying intervals by ear (major/minor sixths, sevenths)
Transcribing melodies, rhythms & chord progressions
Recognizing scales, modes & chord qualities in real songs
Computing harmonic movement & bass lines
Music Factory: complete song analysis & notation

rhythm.title: Rhythm & World Music Patterns
items: Accented arpeggios with irregular groupings & syncopation
Salsa Piano: Tresillo, Claves (3:2, 2:3, Rumba, Bossa)
Building rhythmic phrases with Konnakol (South Indian)
Blues with left-hand Charleston & dominant-7th patterns
Odd metres (7/8, 5/8) & complex accent schemes (3-3-2, 3-3-3-3-4)

classes: 12 Musicianship Classes (Theory, Ear Training, Rhythm, Composition)
features: Live sessions + recordings for every class

coursePreview.title: How Our Intermediate & Advanced Piano Program Works
coursePreview.subtitle: Four Classroom Types, One Goal: Complete Musicianship
coursePreview.description: In this video, Jason explains our unique approach to intermediate and advanced piano training. We focus on growing you as a musician first – through theory, ear training, transcription, rhythm, and notation. Piano skills naturally follow. You'll learn everything by ear, bring your own songs to transcribe, and work across four specialized classrooms: Modular Piano (topic-focused lessons), Music Gym (3D exercises), Theory & Ear Training (notation and rhythm theory), and Music Factory (our signature deep transcription class). This isn't a hobby course – it's a graded certificate program with weekly homework, personalized feedback, and HD recordings of every lesson. Choose from flexible time zones and 32/40/50/65 hour packages over a 6-month semester. No age cap. For students who want to become musicians.

whoIsFor.title: Performers & Improvisers

whoIsFor.title: Trinity/ABRSM Advanced Students

whoIsFor.title: Serious Students (Any Age)
whoIsFor.description: Committed to weekly homework, assignments, grading, and genuine music education - no age limits, just dedication

testimonials.name: Shashank Basavaraj
testimonials.teaser: I think there is no music school in Bangalore that comes up with performance based learning that to in just four months.
testimonials.quote: Hi am learning Piano here. One of my friend suggested Nathaniel School Of Music. Teachers are to help full. I think there is no music school in Bangalore that comes up with performance based learning that to in just four months. I just love jam sessions and reviews given by Jason before the gig. This is the only place to learn music in and around Bangalore.

testimonials.name: Mala Govind
testimonials.teaser: NSM has a very structured way of teaching. Jason is a very good teacher and has taught us in such a way that we understand theory well.
testimonials.quote: Well, at the outset a big thank you to NSM. I have loved being here. When I started I was in doubt as to how I was going to learn piano? NSM has a very structured way of teaching. The faculty is very good. Jason is a very good teacher and has taught us in such a way that we understand theory well. It has given me the opportunity to mix with the youngsters which I enjoyed. I have finished my 2nd level in keyboard and am very much looking forward to level 3. I have also started to respect a song, the musician and the instruments. A big thank you once again.

testimonials.name: Adam Talbot
testimonials.teaser: Jason is an incredible teacher, he has a natural ability to teach complex methods but in a way that is easy to understand.
testimonials.quote: I have been learning piano with Jason Zac at Nathaniel now for over a year after discovering his amazing videos on YouTube. Jason is an incredible teacher, he has a natural ability to teach complex methods but in a way that is easy to understand. As I live abroad, lessons are done online via Zoom and Jason checks in during the week in a private WhatsApp group to review homework, etc, as well as answering any questions I have. I'd highly recommend Jason from beginners through to advanced players, you won't regret it.

testimonials.name: Khushpinder Sangha
testimonials.teaser: I had never played keyboard before in my life but within 8 months time I could pick up quite a bit.
testimonials.quote: I started with Nathaniel's about a year back. The school was recommended by a friend of mine who had done a guitar course here and I'm so glad I took the decision to join it. I did the level 1 vocals, and a level 1 and level 2 in keyboard. The curriculum is very well designed. I loved the theory classes with Jason. Even theory was taught in a very practical manner. Jason's keyboard classes are also the BEST. He is an amazing teacher who makes learning music a joyride. I had never played keyboard before in my life but within 8 months time I could pick up quite a bit. Along with the learning, the experience of jamming with a band, performing at the concert and recording in a studio is something that I loved. Another good thing is they keep having several workshops for those who want to learn more. The school is a great place for anyone who is sincere about music. You will have to put in the hard work and the hours but you will learn a ton.

testimonials.name: Vipul Jain
testimonials.teaser: From knowing nothing in music, to be able to figure out chords for any random song feels amazing.
testimonials.quote: From knowing nothing in music, to be able to figure out chords for any random song feels amazing. It has been an amazing experience at Nathaniel for me. Special thanks to Jason for his amazing way of teaching keyboard. A great aspect of this course is that you get to experience the Jamming experience which is wonderful. Also, you get to play in front of a live audience which can do amazing things to your confidence.

testimonials.name: Vivekananda Roy
testimonials.teaser: An outstanding learning platform with enriching musical environment for all round growth of musicianship.
testimonials.quote: An outstanding learning platform with enriching musical environment for all round growth of musicianship including the main instrument, in my case the piano... an absolute delight learning from Mr Jason who is supremely gifted, talented and super creative musician beyond compare... I wish success to the School and wish more music enthusiasts, eager to learn, get blessed...

faq.question: Who is this program for?
faq.answer: This is for intermediate and advanced piano students who want to grow as complete musicians. Whether you've studied classical piano (Trinity/ABRSM), are a self-taught player, or have completed foundation-level training elsewhere – if you want to understand music theory by ear, transcribe songs, master rhythm, and go deeper than just playing notes, this program is for you. No age cap. We've trained students from teenagers to 75+.

faq.question: What makes this different from other piano courses?
faq.answer: We focus on growing you as a musician first – the piano becomes a byproduct. Instead of just memorizing scales and chords, you'll learn everything by ear. You'll know the sixth of Bb major is G, hear it's a major sixth, and understand how it relates to surrounding notes – all applied to real songs. We use four specialized classrooms: Modular Piano (focused topic lessons), Music Gym (3D exercises for ears, technique, theory), Theory & Ear Training (notation and rhythm theory), and Music Factory (deep transcription analysis). This is serious music education, not a hobby course.

faq.question: What is Music Factory and why is it so important?
faq.answer: Music Factory is our signature transcription class and the most popular among students. You'll set a goal to learn a song, then transcribe every aspect by ear – notation, chord progressions, rhythm patterns, phrase structure. You'll understand WHY the composer chose each chord, not just WHAT they played. Once you understand the entirety of the song, you can then play it. Or you might just enjoy having analyzed a great piece of music. This develops deep listening, analysis, and transcription skills that make you a true musician.

faq.question: Can I bring my own songs to learn?
faq.answer: Absolutely! In fact, we encourage it. You bring your favorite songs to class, and we transcribe them together. Whether it's a Bollywood track, a jazz standard, gospel, pop, or classical piece – you'll learn to analyze and play music that matters to you. This makes the learning experience personal and relevant.

faq.question: How do the class timings work for different time zones?
faq.answer: We offer flexible scheduling across time zones: weekday early mornings, evenings after office hours, and weekend slots (Saturdays and Sundays). You can choose what works best for your schedule. We have students joining from all over the world – India, Singapore, Europe, U.S. – learning at their convenience.

faq.question: What if I miss a class?
faq.answer: Don't worry. Every live session is recorded in HD (almost the same quality as our YouTube videos) and shared privately with you. You'll also receive Jason's handwritten notes, notation, supplementary resources, and practice tracks for every lesson. You can attend live and use the recording for revision, or catch up if you miss a session due to a hectic schedule.

faq.question: Is this a graded course? Will there be homework?
faq.answer: Yes, this is a serious graded certificate program. You'll have weekly homework and assignments. You'll send them to Jason, who will listen, provide personalized feedback, and grade your work. This isn't for hobbyists – it's for students who want to become musicians at any age. By the end of the semester, you'll receive a graded certificate from Nathaniel School of Music.

faq.question: What are the different package options?
faq.answer: You can choose from 32, 40, 50, or 65-hour packages over a 6-month semester. We bill simply by the number of hours. Pick the package that suits your learning goals and time commitment. All packages include live classes, HD recordings, handwritten notes, notation, supplementary resources, and practice tracks.

faq.question: What's the difference between Modular Piano, Music Gym, Theory & Ear Training, and Music Factory?
faq.answer: Modular Piano consists of 2-4 lesson modules focused on specific topics: singing & playing, chord inversions, gospel music, seasonal songs (like Christmas carols). Music Gym offers dedicated 3D exercises to build your ears, technique, and theory with structured practice. Theory & Ear Training classes cover notation, ear training, improvisation, rhythm practice, and staff notation – including rhythm theory, which is often missing from music education. Music Factory is our deep transcription class where you analyze entire songs from notation to chord logic to rhythm structure.

faq.question: Do I need prior piano experience?
faq.answer: Yes. This is an intermediate/advanced program, not for absolute beginners. You should be comfortable with basic piano playing, know some chords and scales, and be ready to dive into theory, ear training, and transcription at a deeper level. If you're a foundation graduate, self-taught, or have studied classical piano and want to understand the 'why' behind music, this is perfect for you.

faq.question: Do you cover rhythm theory?
faq.answer: Yes! Unlike most courses, we teach rhythm as theory – a complete system often missing from music education. You'll learn Konnakol (South Indian rhythmic patterns), Afro-Cuban rhythms, complex metres (7/8, 5/8), irregular groupings, and syncopation. Rhythm is treated as seriously as harmony and melody.

faq.question: Do I get a demo session before enrolling?
faq.answer: We don't offer free demo sessions before enrollment. However, you can explore Jason's teaching style from our YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCCI37YB3l21oq_sLoc92YfA?sub_confirmation=1) or the free tutorials on our website (https://www.nathanielschool.com/free-tutorials). These will give you a clear idea of the quality and depth of our teaching.
